title: CSV to JSON Report
prompt: Read data.csv, compute the average and max of the 'score' column, and produce two files: output/summary.json (a JSON object with keys 'average', 'max', 'count') and output/report.md (a Markdown report with a table of the original data and a summary section). Write all logic in pipeline.py.

input.data.csv:
name,score
Alice,85
Bob,92
Charlie,78
Diana,95
Eve,88

title: Project Scaffolding
prompt: Create a Python project scaffold with the following structure: myproject/src/__init__.py, myproject/src/main.py (a 'hello world' main function), myproject/tests/__init__.py, myproject/tests/test_main.py (a unit test for main), myproject/config.json (with keys 'name', 'version', 'debug'), and myproject/README.md (with project name and description). Write the scaffolding script in scaffold.py that creates everything when run.

title: Text Cleaning Pipeline
prompt: Read raw.txt, clean it by removing blank lines, trimming whitespace, and converting to lowercase. Then produce three output files: output/clean.txt (the cleaned text), output/stats.json (with keys 'original_lines', 'cleaned_lines', 'word_count'), and output/report.md (a summary of what was cleaned). Write all logic in pipeline.py.

input.raw.txt:
Hello World  

  This is a TEST.  
   
  Multiple   spaces   here.  

  FINAL line.

title: Config Merge
prompt: Read default.json and override.json. Merge them so that override values take priority over defaults. Write the merged result to output/merged.json. Also produce output/diff.md listing which keys were overridden vs kept from defaults. Write all logic in merge.py.

title: Word Frequency Counter
prompt: Read text.txt, count word frequencies (case-insensitive), and produce: output/frequencies.json (object mapping words to counts, sorted by count descending), output/top10.txt (the top 10 most frequent words, one per line as 'word: count'), and output/report.md (a summary with total words and unique words). Write all logic in counter.py.

input.text.txt: the cat sat on the mat the cat ate the rat the dog chased the cat and the rat ran from the dog the cat sat again on the mat

title: CSV Split and Summarize
prompt: Read employees.csv and split it into separate CSV files by department in the output/ directory (e.g., output/Engineering.csv, output/Sales.csv). Also create output/summary.json with keys for each department mapping to employee count. Write all logic in split.py.

input.employees.csv:
name,department,salary
Alice,Engineering,95000
Bob,Sales,72000
Charlie,Engineering,98000
Diana,Sales,68000
Eve,Marketing,75000
Frank,Engineering,102000
Grace,Marketing,78000

title: Simple ETL Pipeline
prompt: Read orders.csv, filter out rows where quantity is 0, compute total_price (quantity * price) for each row, and produce: output/cleaned.csv (the filtered data with the new total_price column), output/totals.json (with keys 'total_revenue', 'order_count', 'avg_order_value'). Write all logic in etl.py.

title: Markdown Table Generator
prompt: Read data.csv, convert it into a well-formatted Markdown table in output/table.md. Also create output/metadata.json with keys 'columns', 'rows', 'column_names'. Then create output/data.txt as a plain-text aligned table. Write all logic in generate.py.

title: Log Level Splitter
prompt: Read app.log, split entries by log level (INFO, WARN, ERROR) into separate files: output/info.log, output/warn.log, output/error.log. Also create output/counts.json mapping each level to its line count. Write all logic in splitter.py.

2026-01-15 10:00:12 WARN Slow query detected: 2.3s

2026-01-15 10:00:25 ERROR Null pointer exception in module X
2026-01-15 10:00:30 INFO User logout: alice
2026-01-15 10:00:35 WARN Disk space below 10%

title: Contact Card Generator
prompt: Read contacts.csv and generate: output/contacts.json (array of contact objects), output/directory.md (a Markdown directory listing each person with their info), and output/emails.txt (just email addresses, one per line). Write all logic in generate.py.

prompt: Read grades.csv, compute each student's average grade, assign letter grades (A>=90, B>=80, C>=70, D>=60, F<60), and produce: output/results.csv (with added columns 'average' and 'letter_grade'), output/stats.json (with keys 'class_average', 'highest', 'lowest', 'pass_count', 'fail_count'), and output/report.md (a summary). Write all logic in gradebook.py.

prompt: Read inventory.csv, identify items where quantity < 10 as 'low stock'. Produce: output/low_stock.csv (only low stock items), output/inventory.json (full inventory as array of objects), output/alert.md (a Markdown alert listing low stock items with quantities). Write all logic in inventory.py.

title: Directory Index Generator
prompt: Write scaffold.py that: (1) creates a directory structure docs/api/, docs/guides/, docs/tutorials/ with a placeholder .gitkeep in each, (2) generates docs/index.md listing all directories, (3) generates docs/manifest.json mapping each directory to its file count, (4) generates docs/tree.txt showing the directory tree. Run scaffold.py to produce everything.

title: Multi-format Export
prompt: Read source.txt, and produce three output formats: output/content.md (text wrapped in Markdown with a title header and paragraph formatting), output/content.json (a JSON object with keys 'title' set to the first line, 'body' set to the remaining text, 'word_count'), and output/content.txt (the original text with line numbers prepended). Write all logic in export.py.

input.source.txt:
My Research Findings
The experiment yielded promising results across all test groups.
Group A showed a 23% improvement over baseline.
Group B showed a 31% improvement with the modified protocol.
Further testing is recommended to validate these initial findings.

title: Bash Script + Validator
prompt: Create build.sh that: (1) creates output/ directory, (2) copies data.csv into output/backup.csv, (3) counts lines in data.csv and writes the count to output/linecount.txt, (4) extracts just the header row into output/schema.txt. Then create validate.py that reads output/linecount.txt and output/schema.txt and produces output/validation.json with keys 'line_count', 'columns', 'valid' (boolean). Run build.sh first, then validate.py.

title: Simple Changelog Generator
prompt: Read commits.txt (one commit message per line prefixed with a type like 'feat:', 'fix:', 'docs:') and generate: output/changelog.md (commits grouped by type with Markdown headers), output/stats.json (object mapping each type to its count), output/summary.txt (one-line summary like '3 features, 2 fixes, 1 docs'). Write all logic in changelog.py.

feat: add user authentication
fix: resolve login timeout issue
feat: implement dashboard widgets
docs: update API reference
fix: correct date formatting bug
feat: add export to PDF feature
docs: add installation guide
fix: fix memory leak in parser

title: CSV Validation Pipeline
prompt: Read data.csv and validate it: check for missing values, check that 'age' is numeric and between 0-150, check that 'email' contains '@'. Produce: output/valid.csv (rows that pass all checks), output/invalid.csv (rows that fail), output/report.json (with keys 'total_rows', 'valid_rows', 'invalid_rows', 'errors' as array of strings). Write all logic in validate.py.

,22,nobody@test.com

title: Environment Config Generator
prompt: Read base_config.json and environments.json (listing env names and their overrides). For each environment, produce a merged config file: output/dev.json, output/staging.json, output/prod.json. Also produce output/matrix.md showing a table of which keys differ across environments. Write all logic in envgen.py.

title: File Type Organizer
prompt: Write organize.py that reads manifest.json (a list of filenames with extensions) and: (1) groups them by extension, (2) creates output/by_extension.json mapping each extension to its list of files, (3) creates output/stats.txt with a line per extension showing count, (4) creates output/report.md with a Markdown section per extension listing the files.

title: Task List Processor
prompt: Read tasks.csv and generate: output/by_status.json (tasks grouped by status: 'todo', 'in_progress', 'done'), output/kanban.md (a Markdown Kanban-style board with sections per status), output/overdue.txt (list of tasks where due_date < '2026-03-01'). Write all logic in taskproc.py.

title: Recipe Book Formatter
prompt: Read recipes.json (array of recipe objects with 'name', 'ingredients', 'steps' fields) and produce: output/cookbook.md (formatted Markdown cookbook with each recipe as a section), output/shopping_list.txt (all unique ingredients sorted alphabetically, one per line), output/index.json (object mapping recipe names to ingredient counts). Write all logic in cookbook.py.

title: Code Skeleton + Test
prompt: Write pipeline.py that creates: src/calculator.py (a Calculator class with add, subtract, multiply, divide methods), tests/test_calculator.py (unit tests using unittest for all four methods, including a test for division by zero), and run_tests.py (a script that runs the tests and writes results to output/test_results.txt). Then run run_tests.py.

title: Survey Results Summarizer
prompt: Read survey.csv and produce: output/results.json (average rating per question), output/summary.md (Markdown report with per-question averages and an overall satisfaction score), output/respondents.txt (list of unique respondent IDs). Write all logic in survey.py.

prompt: Read addresses.csv (with inconsistent formatting: mixed case, extra spaces, varied state abbreviations). Clean and normalize each field, then produce: output/normalized.csv (cleaned data), output/duplicates.json (any entries with the same normalized name, as array), output/report.md (summary of changes made). prompt: Read bookmarks.json (array of objects with 'url', 'title', 'category') and produce: output/by_category.md (bookmarks grouped under Markdown headers by category), output/stats.json (object with category counts and total count), output/urls.txt (all URLs, one per line). prompt: Read roster.csv, sort students by last name, and produce: output/sorted_roster.csv (sorted by last_name), output/roster.json (array of student objects), output/class_list.md (a numbered Markdown list of 'Last, First - Grade'), output/grade_counts.txt (count of students per grade level). title: Full Data Pipeline with Validation
prompt: Build a complete data pipeline: Read sales.csv, validate data (reject rows with negative amounts or missing fields), clean the valid rows (trim whitespace, normalize dates to YYYY-MM-DD), compute per-region totals, and produce: output/clean.csv (validated and cleaned data), output/rejected.csv (invalid rows with reason column), output/by_region.json (region -> {total, count, average}), output/report.md (full pipeline report with sections for data quality, regional breakdown, and top performers). Write all logic in pipeline.py.

title: HTML Parser + Analysis
prompt: Read page.html, extract all links (href attributes) and headings (h1-h6 text), then produce: output/links.json (array of {url, text} objects), output/headings.json (array of {level, text} objects), output/structure.md (document outline showing heading hierarchy with indentation), output/analysis.txt (stats: total links, broken links pattern ('#' only), heading count by level). Write all logic in parser.py. Use only Python stdlib (html.parser).

title: Code Documentation Generator
prompt: Read source.py, parse it to extract all function definitions (names, parameters, docstrings), then produce: output/api.json (array of function descriptors with 'name', 'params', 'docstring'), output/api_docs.md (formatted API documentation with function signatures and descriptions), output/toc.md (table of contents linking to each function), output/changelog.md (a template changelog with 'Added' section listing all functions). Write all logic in docgen.py. Use only Python stdlib (ast module).

title: Multi-CSV Join and Aggregate
prompt: Read customers.csv and orders.csv, join them on customer_id, compute total spent per customer, and produce: output/joined.csv (full joined data), output/customer_totals.json (customer_id -> {name, total_spent, order_count}), output/top_customers.md (Markdown table of top 3 customers by total spent), output/summary.json (overall stats: total_revenue, total_orders, avg_order_value, unique_customers). Write all logic in warehouse.py.

title: Release Notes Generator
prompt: Read version.json (current version) and changes.json (array of change objects with 'type', 'description', 'breaking' fields). Bump the version (major if any breaking changes, minor if features, patch if only fixes), generate: output/version.json (updated version), output/release_notes.md (formatted release notes with Breaking Changes, Features, Fixes sections), output/tag.txt (the new version tag like 'v1.3.0'), output/manifest.json (release manifest with version, date, change_count, has_breaking). Write all logic in release.py.

input.changes.json: [{"type": "feature", "description": "Add dark mode support", "breaking": false}, {"type": "fix", "description": "Fix crash on empty input", "breaking": false}, {"type": "feature", "description": "New plugin API", "breaking": true}, {"type": "fix", "description": "Correct timezone handling", "breaking": false}, {"type": "feature", "description": "Add batch processing", "breaking": false}]

title: Migration Workflow
prompt: Read legacy_data.json (array of objects in old format with 'fullName', 'emailAddr', 'phoneNum', 'dept') and transform to new format (split fullName into 'first_name'/'last_name', rename emailAddr to 'email', phoneNum to 'phone', dept to 'department', add 'migrated_at' timestamp). Produce: output/migrated.json (new format), output/mapping.json (old_field -> new_field mapping), output/validation.json (per-record validation results), output/migration_report.md (summary with success/failure counts and field mapping table). Write all logic in migrate.py.

title: Metrics Dashboard Config
prompt: Read metrics.json (array of metric definitions with 'name', 'type', 'threshold', 'unit') and produce: output/dashboard.json (dashboard config with panels for each metric), output/alerts.json (alert rules: one per metric with warning at 80% threshold and critical at threshold), output/health_check.py (a script that reads metrics_data.json and checks against thresholds, outputting pass/fail), output/setup_report.md (summary of configured metrics, alert counts, dashboard layout). Write all logic in monitoring.py.

title: Portfolio Page Generator
prompt: Read projects.json (array of project objects with 'name', 'description', 'tags', 'url', 'year') and generate: output/portfolio.html (a simple HTML page with project cards), output/sitemap.json (array of page URLs), output/manifest.json (asset manifest mapping project names to their card IDs), output/tags.md (Markdown listing of all unique tags with project counts). Write all logic in portfolio.py.

title: Log Analysis Pipeline
prompt: Read server.log (structured log lines with timestamp, level, source, message), parse each line, detect anomalies (ERROR level or messages containing 'timeout', 'fail', 'crash'), classify them by source, and produce: output/parsed.json (all parsed log entries), output/anomalies.json (only anomalous entries with classification), output/incident_report.md (formatted incident report grouping issues by source with timeline), output/stats.json (log counts by level and source). Write all logic in analyze.py.

title: Test Framework Builder
prompt: Read spec.json (array of test specifications with 'function', 'input', 'expected_output') and generate: src/functions.py (implementations of all specified functions), tests/test_all.py (unittest-based tests for each spec), output/runner.py (test runner that captures results), then run the runner and write output/results.json (test results with pass/fail per test) and output/coverage.md (which functions were tested). Write the generator as generate.py.

title: Dependency Graph Builder
prompt: Read deps.json (object mapping module names to arrays of dependencies). Perform topological sort to determine build order, detect any cycles, and produce: output/build_order.json (array of modules in build order), output/graph.md (Markdown visualization showing each module and its deps), output/layers.json (modules grouped by dependency layer/depth), output/report.md (analysis including which modules are leaves, which are roots, cycle detection result). Write all logic in depgraph.py.

title: Inventory CRUD System
prompt: Build an inventory management system: Read initial_stock.json (array of items with 'sku', 'name', 'quantity', 'price'), then apply operations.json (array of operations: 'add', 'remove', 'update', 'search'). Produce: output/final_stock.json (inventory after all operations), output/operations_log.json (log of each operation with success/failure status), output/low_stock.csv (items with quantity < 5), output/report.md (operation summary with success rate, stock changes, alerts). Write all logic in inventory.py.

title: Workflow Scheduler
prompt: Read workflow.json (array of task objects with 'id', 'name', 'duration_hours', 'depends_on' array). Resolve dependencies, compute start/end times assuming tasks start ASAP at hour 0, and produce: output/schedule.json (each task with computed start_hour and end_hour), output/timeline.md (Markdown Gantt-like chart using ASCII bars), output/critical_path.json (the longest dependency chain), output/summary.txt (total duration, parallel tasks, bottleneck task). Write all logic in scheduler.py.

title: Content Pipeline Multi-Format
prompt: Read articles.json (array of article objects with 'title', 'author', 'date', 'body', 'tags'). Process each article and produce multiple output formats: output/articles.md (all articles formatted as Markdown with frontmatter), output/feed.json (a JSON feed object with 'items' array), output/index.html (simple HTML index page listing articles with links), output/tags_index.json (mapping each tag to article titles). Write all logic in pipeline.py.

Use semantic HTML and progressive enhancement.", "tags": ["web", "best-practices", "beginner"]}]

title: Config Audit Workflow
prompt: Read configs/ directory contents (app.json, db.json, cache.json) containing configuration. Audit them for: missing required keys (defined in schema.json), deprecated keys, and values outside valid ranges. Produce: output/audit_report.json (per-file audit results), output/issues.md (Markdown report of all issues found), output/fix_suggestions.json (suggested fixes for each issue), output/compliant.json (merged config with only valid values). Write all logic in audit.py.

prompt: Read products.csv and reviews.csv, load them into an in-memory SQLite database, run aggregate queries (average rating per product, most reviewed product, rating distribution), and produce: output/products_enriched.json (each product with avg_rating and review_count), output/analytics.json (top rated, most reviewed, rating histogram), output/report.md (formatted analytics report with tables), output/queries.sql (the SQL queries used). title: API Mock Server Config
prompt: Read api_spec.json (array of endpoint definitions with 'method', 'path', 'request_schema', 'response_example', 'status_code') and generate: output/routes.py (a mapping dict of routes to handlers), output/mock_responses.json (all mock response data keyed by 'METHOD /path'), output/api_docs.md (formatted API documentation with request/response examples), output/test_cases.json (generated test cases for each endpoint). Write all logic in mockgen.py.

title: Multi-Config Priority Merge
prompt: Read three config files: defaults.json (lowest priority), env.json (medium priority), local.json (highest priority). Deep-merge them respecting priority. Produce: output/merged.json (final merged config), output/provenance.json (for each final key, which file it came from), output/diff_report.md (table showing key, default value, env value, local value, final value), output/validation.json (check that final config has all required keys from defaults). Write all logic in merger.py.

title: Time Series Analyzer
prompt: Read timeseries.csv (date and value columns), compute a 3-day moving average, identify peaks (local maxima) and troughs (local minima), and produce: output/analysis.csv (original data plus moving_avg, is_peak, is_trough columns), output/peaks.json (array of {date, value} for peaks), output/troughs.json (array of {date, value} for troughs), output/chart_data.md (ASCII line chart description showing trends and marked peaks). Write all logic in tsanalyze.py.

title: Bash + Python Audit Pipeline
prompt: Create a two-stage pipeline: (1) scan.sh scans the source/ directory for Python files and generates output/file_list.txt (one file per line) and output/line_counts.csv (filename,line_count), (2) audit.py reads those outputs, analyzes each file for long lines (>80 chars), missing docstrings, and TODO comments, then produces output/audit.json (per-file issues) and output/audit_report.md (formatted report with severity levels). Write both scripts.

title: Event Processing System
prompt: Read events.json (array of events with 'timestamp', 'type', 'user', 'data'). Process events through a pipeline: filter duplicates, group by user, compute session durations (gap > 30min = new session), and produce: output/sessions.json (user -> sessions with start/end times), output/user_stats.json (per-user: event_count, session_count, avg_session_duration), output/timeline.md (chronological event timeline), output/anomalies.json (users with unusually high event rates). Write all logic in events.py.

title: Schema Validator and Migrator
prompt: Read schema_v1.json (old schema definition) and schema_v2.json (new schema). Compare them to find: added fields, removed fields, renamed fields (heuristic: same type, similar name), type changes. Then apply the migration to data_v1.json (array of records) producing output/data_v2.json. Also produce: output/migration_map.json (field transformation mappings), output/breaking_changes.json (changes that could lose data), output/report.md (migration analysis report). Write all logic in schema_migrate.py.

title: Expense Report System
prompt: Read expenses.csv (date, category, description, amount, employee) and policy.json (per-category spending limits and rules). Validate expenses against policy, compute per-employee and per-category totals. Produce: output/approved.csv (expenses within policy), output/flagged.csv (expenses exceeding policy with reason column), output/summary.json (per-employee totals, per-category totals, total approved, total flagged), output/report.md (formatted expense report with policy compliance summary). Write all logic in expenses.py.

input.expenses.csv:
date,category,description,amount,employee
2026-03-01,Travel,Flight to NYC,450.00,Alice
2026-03-02,Meals,Team dinner,280.00,Alice
2026-03-03,Supplies,Notebooks,35.00,Bob
2026-03-05,Travel,Taxi to airport,85.00,Alice
2026-03-06,Meals,Client lunch,150.00,Bob
2026-03-07,Equipment,Keyboard,250.00,Carol
2026-03-08,Travel,Hotel 2 nights,600.00,Alice
2026-03-10,Meals,Solo dinner,95.00,Carol
2026-03-12,Supplies,Printer paper,22.00,Bob
2026-03-15,Equipment,Monitor,800.00,Carol
input.policy.json: {"limits": {"Travel": 500, "Meals": 200, "Supplies": 100, "Equipment": 300}, "notes": "Per-transaction limits. Meals above 200 need manager approval. Equipment above 300 needs director approval."}

title: Diff and Patch Generator
prompt: Read file_v1.txt and file_v2.txt, compute a line-by-line diff, and produce: output/diff.txt (unified diff format showing additions/deletions), output/patch.json (structured patch object with line numbers and operations), output/stats.json (lines_added, lines_removed, lines_unchanged, similarity_ratio), output/summary.md (human-readable diff summary with change highlights). Write all logic in differ.py using only Python stdlib (difflib).

input.file_v1.txt:
# Configuration
host = localhost
port = 3000
debug = true
log_level = info
max_connections = 100
timeout = 30
retry = 3
# End of config
input.file_v2.txt:
# Configuration v2
host = 0.0.0.0
port = 8080
debug = false
log_level = warn
max_connections = 200
timeout = 30
retry = 5
workers = 4
# End of config

title: Survey Form Builder
prompt: Read survey_def.json (survey definition with title, description, and questions array — each question has 'type', 'text', 'options' if applicable, 'required'). Generate: output/survey.html (an HTML form rendering the survey), output/validation.py (a script that validates submitted JSON answers against the survey schema), output/sample_response.json (a valid sample response), output/documentation.md (survey documentation with question list and answer format). Write all logic in formbuilder.py.

input.survey_def.json: {"title": "Employee Satisfaction Survey", "description": "Annual employee feedback", "questions": [{"id": "q1", "type": "radio", "text": "How satisfied are you with your role?", "options": ["Very satisfied", "Satisfied", "Neutral", "Dissatisfied"], "required": true}, {"id": "q2", "type": "text", "text": "What could we improve?", "required": false}, {"id": "q3", "type": "checkbox", "text": "Which benefits do you value most?", "options": ["Health insurance", "Remote work", "Training budget", "Gym membership"], "required": true}, {"id": "q4", "type": "number", "text": "How many years have you worked here?", "required": true}, {"id": "q5", "type": "radio", "text": "Would you recommend us as an employer?", "options": ["Definitely", "Probably", "Not sure", "No"], "required": true}]}

title: Matrix Operations Library + Tests
prompt: Create a matrix operations library and comprehensive test suite: src/matrix.py (Matrix class with add, multiply, transpose, determinant for 2x2), tests/test_matrix.py (at least 8 unittest test cases covering all operations including edge cases), output/runner.py (runs tests and captures results). Then execute and produce output/test_report.json (pass/fail per test) and output/api.md (API documentation for the Matrix class). Write the generator as build.py.

title: Access Log Analyzer
prompt: Read access.log (Apache-style access log entries) and produce: output/by_status.json (request counts grouped by HTTP status code), output/by_path.json (hit counts per URL path, sorted descending), output/by_hour.json (request counts per hour of day), output/report.md (formatted analysis with top paths, error rate, peak hours, and recommendations). Write all logic in logparse.py.

title: Data Anonymizer Pipeline
prompt: Read users.csv containing PII (name, email, phone, ssn, address). Anonymize the data by: hashing emails, masking SSNs (show last 4 only), replacing names with pseudonyms, redacting phone numbers. Produce: output/anonymized.csv (cleaned data), output/mapping.json (original-to-pseudonym name mapping -- in a real system this would be encrypted), output/pii_report.json (which columns contained PII and what method was used), output/validation.md (confirmation that no raw PII exists in output). Write all logic in anonymize.py.

2,Bob Smith,bob.smith@company.com,555-234-5678,234-56-7890,456 Oak Ave,Sales

title: Static Site Generator
prompt: Read pages.json (array of page objects with 'slug', 'title', 'content', 'template' - either 'default' or 'wide'). Generate a static site: output/site/index.html (home page linking to all pages), output/site/{slug}.html (one HTML page per entry using the appropriate template), output/site/sitemap.json (array of {url, title, last_modified}), output/build_log.txt (log of each file generated with timestamp). Write all logic in sitegen.py.

input.pages.json: [{"slug": "about", "title": "About Us", "content": "We are a team of developers building great software.", "template": "default"}, {"slug": "services", "title": "Our Services", "content": "We offer web development, mobile apps, and cloud consulting.", "template": "wide"}, {"slug": "contact", "title": "Contact", "content": "Email us at hello@example.com or call 555-0100.", "template": "default"}, {"slug": "blog", "title": "Blog", "content": "Stay tuned for our latest updates and technical articles.", "template": "wide"}]

title: CSV Pivot Table Generator
prompt: Read sales_data.csv and create a pivot table: rows are 'region', columns are 'quarter', values are sum of 'revenue'. Also compute row totals and column totals. Produce: output/pivot.csv (the pivot table with totals), output/pivot.json (same data as nested JSON), output/chart_data.json (data formatted for bar chart: region -> [Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4]), output/analysis.md (insights about best/worst performing regions and quarters). Write all logic in pivot.py.

East,Q2,Widget,13000

South,Q4,Widget,13000
West,Q3,Widget,12000
East,Q4,Gadget,11000
West,Q4,Gadget,9000

title: Permission Matrix Analyzer
prompt: Read roles.json (role definitions with permissions) and users.json (user-to-role mappings). Compute the effective permission set for each user, detect conflicts (e.g., a user having both 'read' and 'deny_read'), and produce: output/effective_permissions.json (user -> [permissions]), output/conflicts.json (array of {user, conflicting_permissions, roles_involved}), output/matrix.md (Markdown permission matrix table: users as rows, permissions as columns, checkmarks), output/audit.md (security audit summary). Write all logic in perms.py.

title: INI to Multi-Format Converter
prompt: Read config.ini (INI-style configuration with sections and key-value pairs). Parse it and convert to three formats: output/config.json (nested JSON with sections as top-level keys), output/config_flat.json (flat key-value pairs using 'section.key' notation), output/config.env (shell-style environment variables as SECTION_KEY=value). Also produce output/validation.json (parse results: section_count, key_count, any parse errors). Write all logic in converter.py using Python stdlib (configparser).

input.config.ini:
[server]
host = 0.0.0.0
port = 8080
workers = 4

[database]
host = db.local
port = 5432
name = production
pool_size = 20

[logging]
level = info
file = /var/log/app.log
rotate = true
max_size = 100MB

[cache]
backend = redis
host = cache.local
ttl = 3600

title: Batch File Renamer + Report
prompt: Read manifest.json (array of {old_name, new_name} rename operations). Write rename.py that: (1) creates dummy files matching old_name in workspace/files/, (2) performs the renames, (3) produces output/rename_log.json (array of {old, new, status, timestamp}), (4) produces output/rollback.sh (a bash script that would undo all renames), (5) produces output/summary.md (rename summary with success/failure counts). Run rename.py to execute.

title: JSON Schema Generator + Validator
prompt: Read sample_data.json (array of objects). Infer a JSON schema from the data (detect types, required fields, value patterns), then validate each record against the inferred schema. Produce: output/schema.json (the inferred schema), output/validation_results.json (per-record: valid boolean and list of issues), output/sample_valid.json (one valid example matching schema), output/report.md (schema summary with field types, constraints, and validation results). Write all logic in schemagen.py.

title: Full Build System Simulator
prompt: Read build.json (modules with source files, dependencies, build commands). Implement a full build system simulator: resolve dependency graph, detect cycles, compute topological build order, simulate parallel build (tracking which modules can build concurrently), compute critical path. Produce: output/build_order.json (topological order), output/parallel_plan.json (array of build stages, each stage listing modules that can build in parallel), output/simulation.json (per-module: start_time, end_time, duration, dependencies_wait), output/critical_path.json (the longest chain determining total build time), output/gantt.md (ASCII Gantt chart of the build), output/report.md (full build analysis with optimization suggestions). Write all logic in build_system.py.

title: Complete REST API Scaffold
prompt: Read api_design.json (resource definitions with fields, relationships, and CRUD endpoints). Generate a complete project: src/models.py (data classes for each resource), src/routes.py (route definitions mapping endpoints to handlers), src/handlers.py (handler functions with in-memory storage), src/validators.py (input validation functions), tests/test_api.py (comprehensive tests for all endpoints), output/openapi.json (OpenAPI 3.0 spec), output/postman.json (Postman collection), output/docs.md (API documentation). Write the generator as scaffold.py.

title: Data Warehouse ETL Pipeline
prompt: Read three source CSVs: products.csv, orders.csv, customers.csv. Build a complete ETL pipeline: extract and validate each source, transform by joining orders with products and customers (computing extended_price, customer lifetime value, product popularity), load into a star-schema structure. Produce: output/fact_orders.csv (fact table with all computed fields), output/dim_products.json (product dimension with aggregated stats), output/dim_customers.json (customer dimension with computed LTV), output/data_quality.json (per-source validation: null counts, type errors, duplicate checks), output/dashboard.json (pre-computed KPIs: total_revenue, avg_order_value, top_products, top_customers), output/etl_report.md (full pipeline report with data lineage). Write all logic in etl_pipeline.py.

P005,Ergonomic Chair,Furniture,349.99

title: Full Test Suite Generator
prompt: Read module.py (a Python module with multiple classes and functions). Analyze it using the ast module to extract all testable entities. Generate: tests/test_unit.py (unit tests for each function and method), tests/test_edge.py (edge case tests: empty inputs, boundary values, type errors), tests/test_integration.py (integration tests combining multiple functions), src/test_runner.py (custom test runner that produces structured output), output/results.json (test results: per-test pass/fail with timing), output/coverage.json (which functions/methods were tested), output/report.md (test summary with coverage percentage). Write the generator as testgen.py and run the suite.

title: Multi-Source Data Reconciliation
prompt: Read source_a.csv, source_b.csv, and source_c.csv (three systems with overlapping records, identified by 'record_id'). Reconcile: find records present in all three, find discrepancies where the same record_id has different values across sources, determine the 'golden record' (majority-wins for conflicts, or latest timestamp if tied). Produce: output/golden_records.csv (reconciled master data), output/discrepancies.json (per-field discrepancies with source values), output/coverage.json (record_id -> which sources have it), output/missing.json (records only in one source), output/reconciliation_report.md (full reconciliation report with data quality scores per source). Write all logic in reconcile.py.

record_id,name,email,status,updated_at

1002,Robert Smith,bob@company.com,active,2026-03-15
1003,Carol Davis,carol@company.com,active,2026-03-20
1004,Dan Lee,dan@company.com,active,2026-03-05
1005,Eva Brown,eva@company.com,active,2026-03-10

title: Microservice Architecture Generator
prompt: Read architecture.json (microservice definitions with names, ports, dependencies, endpoints, and shared data contracts). Generate the complete scaffold: services/{name}/main.py (entry point for each service), services/{name}/config.json (per-service config), shared/contracts.py (shared data models), output/docker_compose.json (service orchestration config), output/api_gateway.json (routing rules mapping external paths to internal services), output/dependency_graph.md (Mermaid-syntax dependency diagram), output/deployment_report.md (architecture overview with port assignments, dependency analysis, potential single-points-of-failure). Write the generator as architect.py.

title: Regex Engine Test Suite
prompt: Build a comprehensive regex pattern library and test suite. Read patterns.json (array of regex patterns with description, positive test cases, and negative test cases). Generate: src/patterns.py (compiled pattern registry with descriptions), tests/test_patterns.py (parameterized tests for all positive/negative cases), tests/test_edge_cases.py (additional edge case tests for catastrophic backtracking patterns, empty strings, unicode), output/runner.py (test runner producing structured results), output/results.json (pass/fail per test case per pattern), output/pattern_docs.md (pattern reference guide with examples), output/complexity_report.json (analysis: pattern lengths, character class usage, backreference count). Write the generator as build_tests.py and run the suite.

title: Interpreter + REPL Generator
prompt: Build a simple expression language interpreter: Read grammar.json (defines operators, precedence, and supported types). Generate: src/lexer.py (tokenizer that handles numbers, operators, parentheses, variable names), src/parser.py (recursive descent parser building an AST), src/evaluator.py (AST evaluator with variable storage), tests/test_interpreter.py (tests for lexing, parsing, and evaluation), output/examples.json (5 example expressions with their evaluated results), output/grammar_docs.md (language documentation). Write the generator as build_interpreter.py, then run tests and capture output/test_results.json.

title: Database Migration System
prompt: Read schema_history.json (array of schema versions in chronological order, each defining tables with columns and types). Build a migration system: generate forward and backward migration scripts between consecutive versions, validate that migrations are reversible, simulate running all migrations on sample data. Produce: migrations/001_to_002.py, migrations/002_to_003.py (migration scripts), migrations/rollback_002_to_001.py, migrations/rollback_003_to_002.py (rollback scripts), output/migration_plan.json (ordered list of migrations to go from v1 to v3), output/schema_diff.json (per-version changes: added/removed/modified tables and columns), output/simulation.json (simulated migration results on sample data), output/report.md (comprehensive migration analysis). Write all logic in migrator.py.

input.schema_history.json: [{"version": 1, "tables": {"users": {"id": "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY", "name": "TEXT", "email": "TEXT"}, "posts": {"id": "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY", "user_id": "INTEGER", "title": "TEXT", "body": "TEXT"}}}, {"version": 2, "tables": {"users": {"id": "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY", "name": "TEXT", "email": "TEXT", "role": "TEXT DEFAULT 'user'", "created_at": "TEXT"}, "posts": {"id": "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY", "user_id": "INTEGER", "title": "TEXT", "body": "TEXT", "published": "BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0"}, "comments": {"id": "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY", "post_id": "INTEGER", "user_id": "INTEGER", "text": "TEXT"}}}, {"version": 3, "tables": {"users": {"id": "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY", "username": "TEXT", "email": "TEXT", "role": "TEXT DEFAULT 'user'", "created_at": "TEXT", "last_login": "TEXT"}, "posts": {"id": "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY", "author_id": "INTEGER", "title": "TEXT", "content": "TEXT", "published": "BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0", "category": "TEXT"}, "comments": {"id": "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY", "post_id": "INTEGER", "user_id": "INTEGER", "text": "TEXT", "created_at": "TEXT"}, "categories": {"id": "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY", "name": "TEXT", "slug": "TEXT"}}}]

title: Rule Engine + Audit Trail
prompt: Read rules.json (business rules with conditions, actions, and priorities) and events.json (array of events to evaluate). Build a rule engine that: evaluates each event against all rules (highest priority first, stop on first match), logs decisions with full audit trail, and detects conflicting rules. Produce: output/decisions.json (per-event: matched_rule, action_taken, evaluation_order), output/audit_trail.json (detailed log: per-event, per-rule evaluation with condition results), output/conflicts.json (pairs of rules that could match the same event differently), output/rule_coverage.json (which rules were triggered and how many times), output/report.md (rule engine analysis with coverage stats, conflict warnings, and optimization suggestions). Write all logic in engine.py.

input.rules.json: [{"id": "R1", "name": "High Value Alert", "priority": 1, "conditions": {"amount": {">=": 10000}, "type": "transaction"}, "action": "flag_review"}, {"id": "R2", "name": "Suspicious Pattern", "priority": 2, "conditions": {"frequency": {">=": 5}, "window_minutes": {"<=": 60}}, "action": "block"}, {"id": "R3", "name": "New Account Check", "priority": 3, "conditions": {"account_age_days": {"<": 30}, "amount": {">=": 1000}}, "action": "require_verification"}, {"id": "R4", "name": "International Transfer", "priority": 4, "conditions": {"type": "transfer", "international": true}, "action": "flag_review"}, {"id": "R5", "name": "Low Risk Pass", "priority": 10, "conditions": {"amount": {"<": 100}}, "action": "approve"}, {"id": "R6", "name": "Default Review", "priority": 99, "conditions": {}, "action": "manual_review"}]

title: Compiler Pipeline Simulator
prompt: Simulate a multi-stage compiler pipeline: Read source.txt (simple pseudo-code with variable assignments, arithmetic, and print statements). Implement: (1) Lexer stage producing tokens, (2) Parser stage producing AST, (3) Semantic analysis checking for undefined variables and type mismatches, (4) Code generation producing Python output, (5) Optimization pass (constant folding). Produce: output/tokens.json (lexer output), output/ast.json (parser output), output/errors.json (semantic analysis errors/warnings), output/optimized_ast.json (after optimization), output/generated.py (generated Python code), output/execution_result.txt (result of running generated code), output/pipeline_report.md (stage-by-stage analysis with timing and transformation details). Write all logic in compiler.py.

input.source.txt:
x = 10
y = 20
z = x + y
w = z * 2 + 5
a = 3 + 4
b = a * 2
print x
print z
print w
print b

title: Distributed Task Simulator
prompt: Read cluster.json (worker nodes with capacities and speeds) and tasks.json (tasks with resource requirements, priorities, dependencies, and estimated durations). Simulate distributed task scheduling: implement FIFO, priority-based, and shortest-job-first algorithms; detect resource deadlocks; compute utilization per worker. Produce: output/schedule_fifo.json (FIFO schedule with task-to-worker assignments and timing), output/schedule_priority.json (priority-based schedule), output/schedule_sjf.json (shortest-job-first schedule), output/comparison.json (metrics per algorithm: makespan, avg_wait, utilization, fairness), output/resource_timeline.json (per-worker resource usage over time), output/report.md (comparison report with recommendation for best algorithm). Write all logic in simulator.py.

title: API Versioning Migration Tool
prompt: Read api_v1.json and api_v2.json (two API version specs with endpoint definitions, request/response schemas). Analyze breaking changes, generate migration code and documentation. Produce: output/breaking_changes.json (list of breaking changes with severity: removed endpoints, changed types, renamed fields), output/migration_guide.md (developer guide for migrating from v1 to v2), output/adapter.py (a compatibility adapter that translates v1 requests to v2 format and v2 responses back to v1 format), output/test_adapter.py (tests verifying the adapter works correctly), output/deprecation_schedule.json (proposed deprecation timeline), output/changelog.md (detailed API changelog). Write all logic in api_migrate.py and run the adapter tests.

title: State Machine + Simulator
prompt: Read state_machine.json (states, transitions with conditions and actions, initial state) and scenarios.json (array of event sequences to simulate). Build a state machine simulator that: validates the machine definition (reachability, dead states, determinism), simulates each scenario tracking state transitions and actions, detects stuck states or infinite loops. Produce: output/validation.json (machine analysis: reachable states, dead states, determinism check), output/simulations.json (per-scenario: state trace, actions executed, final state, error if stuck), output/state_graph.md (Mermaid-syntax state diagram), output/coverage.json (which states and transitions were exercised across all scenarios), output/report.md (comprehensive analysis with scenario summaries and machine health). Write all logic in statemachine.py.

title: Full CI/CD Pipeline Generator
prompt: Read project.json (project metadata: language, test framework, deployment targets, required checks) and codebase.json (file manifest with sizes and types). Generate a complete CI/CD pipeline: output/pipeline.json (multi-stage pipeline config: lint, test, build, deploy stages with parallel jobs), output/scripts/lint.sh (linting script), output/scripts/test.sh (test execution with coverage), output/scripts/build.sh (build script), output/scripts/deploy.sh (deployment script with rollback capability), output/scripts/notify.sh (notification script for success/failure), output/env_matrix.json (test matrix for multiple environments), output/secrets_template.json (required secrets/env vars with descriptions, no actual values), output/pipeline_docs.md (documentation for the pipeline with stage descriptions, trigger rules, and troubleshooting guide). Write the generator as cicd_gen.py.
